test.command: cargo test -j 4 && (cd src-tauri && cargo test -j 4) && (cd frontend && npm test -- --run)

blockingIssues.description: The feature contract requires one interactiveChecks pass/fail record per fulfilled assertion (`features.json:207`), but this handoff provides grouped checks and explicitly calls out partial evidence (`successState: partial` at line 6; partial-evidence notes at lines 11, 78, 94, and 118). As written, coverage for all fulfilled VAL-* assertions cannot be verified from this feature handoff.

SummaryStats now renders the summary glyph twice: once in `title` (lines 134-139) and again via SectionCard `icon` (lines 144-148). The pre-refactor header had a single icon, so this is a visible parity regression.

SectionCard hardcodes neutral title/description colors (`text-gray-*`) regardless of tone (lines 71-72). In ResultsStep read-only mode (`tone="info"`), this drops the previous sky-emphasis typography (previously `text-sky-*`), so the extracted card does not preserve UI parity.

Feature expectedBehavior requires all three App*.test.tsx suites to import shared scaffolding, but this file still defines its own duplicated vi.hoisted mock block (and local tauri/FileSelector mocks at lines 29 and 46) instead of importing shared fixtures.

Feature contract explicitly targets frontend/src/test/fixtures.ts and imports from fixtures.ts, but implementation created fixtures.tsx and consumers import './test/fixtures.tsx' instead (e.g., App.file-selection-navigation.test.tsx:4, App.results-navigation.test.tsx:4).

The search input is controlled by `searchQuery` (`value={searchQuery}`) while `setSearchQuery` is only called inside `startTransition` in `onChange`. Transition updates are non-urgent and are not safe for the controlling state of text inputs; this can make typing laggy/inconsistent on heavy renders. Use an urgent input state for `value` and transition/defer only the expensive filtering state.

Tauri mode now calls `invoke('delete_session', { sessionId })`, but there is no matching Tauri command implementation/registration. `src-tauri/src/main.rs` invoke handler list (lines 275-284) includes create/load/compare/export/pair-order/snapshot commands but no `delete_session`; reset will throw an unknown-command error on desktop and cannot complete the intended delete-then-create flow.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit checks for frontend↔Tauri command parity: whenever services/tauri.ts introduces a new invoke command, require verification that matching #[tauri::command] handler and tauri::generate_handler! registration exist.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature m6-wire-delete-session-on-reset passed frontend tests but scrutiny found invoke("delete_session") without a registered Rust command, causing a desktop-runtime blocker.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document safe useTransition patterns for controlled inputs: keep input value updates urgent and transition only derived/expensive computations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature m6-use-transition-results-table put controlled input state updates inside startTransition, causing a blocking responsiveness/correctness issue.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit fallback for UI validation startup when cargo run fails with AddrInUse: verify health on expected port and reuse the healthy server instead of treating it as a procedure deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature m6-a11y-and-footer-polish recorded AddrInUse while still validating successfully against an already-running healthy server.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Resolve contradiction between validation-only features that forbid source edits and worker success contracts that require a commit; provide a sanctioned no-code success path for such features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m6-full-surface-ui-validation was marked completed with a partial handoff due to this contract conflict and could not satisfy per-assertion evidence requirements cleanly.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require mission.md and features.json acceptance criteria to stay in lockstep (single canonical source or automated consistency check) before assigning workers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Scrutiny observations found conflicting expectations in M6 artifacts (e.g., React compiler cleanup scope; fixture extension/path expectations; reducer helper verification wording).

rejectedObservations.observation: Icon module placement mismatch between architecture docs and feature contract.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in review set and scoped to mission-document drift; no immediate operational impact on validator flow.

rejectedObservations.observation: Rust-worker strictness mismatch from a single handoff (missing src-tauri fmt/clippy in final verification command).
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous single-sample process observation; not enough evidence of systemic drift within this milestone.

rejectedObservations.observation: Frontend-worker user-testing.md read step not reflected in one transcript skeleton.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous and potentially instrumentation-related; insufficient evidence to mandate guidance change.

rejectedObservations.observation: TDD rigidity concern for pure fixture-dedup features.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate with existing policy debate and not directly causal for a blocking product defect in this milestone.

rejectedObservations.observation: Need dedicated assertion-report template for very high-cardinality UI sweeps.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate of the broader validation-only feature contract conflict captured in suggested guidance updates.
